title: CSS Box Model
description: Content, padding, border, and margin — and how `box-sizing` changes what `width` actually means. Includes computing rendered sizes by hand, which is the fastest way to make the model stick.
author: Quizbun

questions.title: From the inside out, what is the order of the box model layers?
questions.explanation: Padding is the space *inside* the border, between it and the content; margin is the space *outside* the border, between the element and its neighbors. A reliable memory hook: padding inherits the element's background color, margin is always transparent — so if the colored area grows, you changed padding.

questions.title: With the default `box-sizing: content-box`, an element has `width: 200px`, `padding: 20px`, and `border: 5px solid`. How wide is the rendered border box, in pixels?
questions.description: Enter a number only, without the `px` unit.
questions.explanation: Under `content-box`, the `width` property sizes only the content area, so padding and border are added on top: 200 + 20 + 20 (left and right padding) + 5 + 5 (left and right border) = **250px**. This additive behavior is exactly why most codebases switch to `border-box`, where the math you write is the size you see.

questions.title: What does `box-sizing: border-box` change?

options.text: `width` and `height` now include padding and border, so the content area shrinks to fit
`width` and `height` now include the margin as well
It removes the border from layout calculations entirely
It makes padding overlap with neighboring elements

questions.explanation: With `border-box`, declaring `width: 200px` means the border box — content plus padding plus border — is 200px, and the browser shrinks the content area to make that true. Margin is never included in either model; it is always space outside the element. The near-universal reset `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }` makes sizing predictable across components.

questions.title: Two stacked paragraphs have `margin-bottom: 30px` and `margin-top: 20px`. In normal flow, how much vertical space separates them, in pixels?
questions.description: Enter a number only, without the `px` unit.
questions.explanation: Adjacent vertical margins in normal flow **collapse**: instead of adding up (50px), the larger one wins, so the gap is 30px. This only happens to vertical margins of block elements in normal flow — flex and grid items never collapse margins, which is one reason layouts behave differently after switching a container to `display: flex`.
